title: Petition to Amphicles the hypomnematographos
summary: The priests of Souchos at Crocodilopolis wrote this petition to complain about the inhabitants of the neighbouring town of Harmonthis, who appear to have been troublesome during this period. They refer especially to the damage done to the property of the king and their temple.

title: Petition
summary: From cartonnage. Cursive hand, same as BGU IV 1060.

title: Repayment of Loan
summary: A receipt issued by Sarapion, senator of Antino�polis, to Isidorus, son of Ptolemaeus, for 30 silver talents which Chaeremon, agent of Sarapion, had loaned to Isidorus under a verbal agreement.

title: Letter from Philoxenos to Zenon
summary: Philoxenes, beginning his letter with a pleasant variation on the usual form of greeting, asks Zenon, as he had asked him once before, to give Exakon two jars full of pressed grapes and pips to enable him and his companions to make themselves some sour wine.

title: Fragment of a petition from Chesthotes (?) to Zenon
summary: The writer had apparently lent 284 drachmae, the loan being confirmed by the royal oath, to another Egyptian on condition that if he did not return the money by a certain time he would be liable to exaction.  The money not having been repaid, Zenon is asked to write to the proper authorities.

title: Fragment of a letter from Horos to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter from Horos to Zenon.

title: Fragment of a letter from Horos to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter from Horos to Zenon.

title: Memorandum from Horos to Zenon
summary: Hedylos the architect had forbidden Horos to attend to the works, but the latter insists that it is his business to keep an account of the bricks provided by the bricklayers.

title: Two epitaphs on a dog
summary: An epitaph in elegiacs, followed by another in iambics, on an Indian dog called Tauron who had been killed in defending Zenon against the attack of a wild boar, but not before he had laid his adversary low.

title: Fragments of verse with musical notation
summary: The earliest Greek document with musical notation.  Perhaps part of an exercise or excerpt written by a pupil on an odd piece of papyrus.  Below are some large letters and combinations of letters, probably drawn without any definite meaning.

title: List of words and phrases
summary: A list, composed largely of literary words which would not have been used in ordinary correspondence or conversation.  Several of them are rare and one at least is new.

title: Hexameter
summary: An hexameter, written on a scrap of papyrus, perhaps by a schoolboy.

title: Part of a list of stores
summary: The earliest dated piece in the Cairo Zenon archive.  It contains a list of goods, probably beloning to the stores of Apollonios and imported by his agents, - wine, honey, incense, myrrh and other products of Arabia.

title: Loan of Wheat
summary: A loan of six artabas of wheat to Heras, son of Melas, from Isidorus, son of Ptolemaeus, and his associates in the sitologia of Karanis.  Interest of 50 per cent, i.e. three artabas, is fixed at the time of the loan, and Heras must therefore return nine artabas in Pauni (May/June) of the same year.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A fragment of a letter that seems to have been written in Palestine, by one of Zenon's subordinates, such as Krotos.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A fragment of a letter.

title: Fragment of an account
summary: A number of small fragments of accounts, written on both sides of the papyrus.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A fragment of a letter with a copy of another letter subjoined.  The writer was engaged in equipping a ship, for which purpose he required the services of an educated slave.  He speaks of certain men of whom some had been drafted into the kun�goi and others assigned to some other duty.

title: Letter from Noumenios to Zenon
summary: Noumenios, who had been ordered by Apollonios to meet him in Memphis by a certain date, writes to ask when Apollonios intends to sail up from Mendes and where he has decided to celebrate the king's birthday and on what day in the Egyptian calendar the festival and sacrifice will take place.

title: Account of money spent on horses
summary: A description of a journey from Alexandria giving details of the sum spent on fodder and ferries.

title: Letter from Melanippos to Zenon
summary: A letter nearly identical with PCZ.I.59101,  stating that a certain Ptolemaios (?) was coming to see Apollonios, and Zenon is asked to assist him.

title: Letter from Eunikos to Apollonios
summary: PCZ.I.59099 is a letter to Zenon on the same subject and is couched in almost the same words, announcing the dispatch of a consignment of fruit of various kinds.  No doubt the second letter was given to him at the same time in order that he might present it to Apollonios.

title: Fragment of two drafts of letters
summary: The first fragment is the end of a draft of PCZ.I.59546; the second is written in a different hand, with abbreviations and other signs of haste.

title: Letter to Panakestor
summary: This is evidently the letter of which PCZ.IV.59545 was the draft; possibly from Zenon; a reminder to Panakestor of an order for him to provide byres for six yoke of oxen sent by Artemidoros.

title: Lease of Land
summary: A lease of land, where the rent has been paid in advance.

title: Letter from Amyntas to Apollonios
summary: Announcing the despatch of certain comestibles from Alexandria, obviously the consignment mentioned in PCZ.I.59110 and IV.59548.

title: Account of wine
summary: An account giving the quantities of wine issued daily for about ten days to certain persons travelling in the retinue of Apollonios.  Obviously referring to the same consignment as mentioned in PCZ.I.59110 and IV.59547.

title: Duplicate receipt
summary: A duplicate receipt.

title: Duplicate receipt
summary: A duplicate receipt.

title: Duplicate receipt
summary: A duplicate receipt.

title: Duplicate receipt
summary: Part of a duplicate receipt.

title: Letter from Theodoros to Zenon
summary: Theodoros asks Zenon to see to the despatch of some lead, to help Sosistratos (?) to recover four drachmae from Damis, being the tax on the wine, and to give him the list of the mattresses handed over.

title: Fragment of a letter from Panakestor
summary: Possibly connected with PCZ.II.59165, in which Apollonios gives orders about the feeding of calves.

title: Fragment of a letter from Artemidoros
summary: A fragment of a letter from Artemidoros, the physician in Alexandria, probably asking Zenon to do some service for him at Philadelphia.
date_text: Date of reception: August - September, 255 B.C.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A fragment of a letter.

title: Lease of Land
summary: A lease in the form of an application addressed by Isidorus, son of Ptolemaeus, to Zoilus, son of Apollonius, and approved by the latter in a subscription written in his own hand.  The area leased comprised three parcels of 4, 4 1/2 and 1 1/2 arouras resp

title: Letter from Apollonios to Zenon (?)
summary: A fragment of a letter from Apollonios to Zenon (?).

title: Fragments of a letter
summary: Two fragments of a letter.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A fragment of a letter from Apollonios (?).

title: Fragment of a letter from Apollonios to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter from Apollonios to Zenon, giving instructions about the goods which Zenon was to send to Alexandria to be presented to the king at the festival of Isis.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter to Zenon.

title: Fragments of a register of letters
summary: Apparently from a copy of a series of letters received by Zenon from Apollonios.  Written in columns.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter to Zenon.

title: Fragment of a letter or memorandum
summary: A fragment of a letter or memorandum, stating that Zenon (?) was to write under the account an order to pay the sum specified.  It probably consisted of taxes due to the Crown.

title: Heading of an account
summary: The heading of an account, probably Zenon's private account.

title: Memorandum to Zenon from Palous
summary: A memorandum giving a list of fittings belonging to a boat.  Below is an apparently unrelated demotic text dated 3rd (?) Thoth, year 36.

title: Lease of Land
summary: Isidorus, son of Ptolemaeus, offers to lease from Nemesinus, councilor and former exegetes of Arsino�, four arouras of wheat land for a period of three years and to pay an annual rent of 9 1/2 artabas, on condition that the lessor pay all the public dues.

title: Part of a money account
summary: Part of a money account, mentioning Aristandros as the oikonomos of the Arsinoite province, coupled with Python, the royal banker in Krokodilopolis, recipients of the Government dues paid through Zenon.

title: Fragment of a corn account
summary: Possibly a register of the receipts given by persons to whom corn has been delivered from a granary.

title: Account of household wheat
summary: Fragments of a long account of household wheat.

title: Letter from Ammonios to Zenon
summary: Written on the 7th of Thoth to inform Zenon, perhaps in reply to a query, that certain quantities of wheat and olyra had been delivered in Pharmouthi and Pachon to the public granary in Syron Kome on behalf of his employee Eutychides.  Traces of deleted demotic under the Greek text.

title: Letter to Zenon
summary: A friend in Alexandria writes that he can give Zenon no definite news about the matter in which he is interested, but asks him to buy the cushions and the carpets which Neon the physician is impatiently awaiting.  He adds the significant remark that Neon is in favour with the king.

title: Letter to Philon
summary: A request to Philon to confer with Eukles about the measurements of certain lands.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A fragment of a letter.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A fragment of a letter.  The handwriting resembles that of some of the letters of Amyntas.

title: Letter to Zenon
summary: A letter to Zenon conveying compliments and good wishes.

title: Letter to Apollonios
summary: A friend informs Apollonios that he is ready to despatch the horses, the she-goat and the lamb, but asks him first to send an agent to see if they are satisfactory.

title: Lease of Land
summary: Both the top and the bottom of this papyrus are torn away, and with them has gone every chance of recovering the names of the lessor and the lessee.  The object of the lease is five arouras of private land at Kerkesoucha and two arouras at Ptolemais Nea.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A complaint that the writer's yearly allowance of corn had been reduced from 100 artabs to 70.

title: Letter to Zenon
summary: The writer asks Zenon to send hom a certain Mikkalion whom he had taken under his protection.

title: Letter to Python and Zenon
summary: From a lady, probably a relative or at least a member of the same Carian circle, asking Python and Zenon to look after Panakestor, probably her son or her husband, whom she has left in their charge.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: An expression of good wishes for the health of Zenon and one of his companions, apparently written by a young relative.

title: Fragment of a letter from Apollonios
summary: A fragment of a letter from Apollonios (?).

title: Fragment of a letter from Apollonios to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter from Apollonios to Zenon.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A fragment of a letter from Apollonios (?).

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A fragment of a letter about a despatch of provisions for the table, and of wood and charcoal.

title: Draft of a letter
summary: A draft of a letter, written in Zenon's usual hand.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A fragment of a letter, containing references to the purchase of horses, whether for ordinary use or for racing, and to the training of promising boys in the gymnasium.

title: Lease of Land
summary: An offer to lease five arouras of the arable land administered by the village of Karanis, for one year, free of rent but with the obligation to discharge all taxes thereon in money and in kind.  The offer is made by Capito, son of Heliodorus, a resident of Arsino�, and Ptolemaeus, son of Ptollarion, a resident of Karanis.

title: Fragment of a letter to Aristandros
summary: A fragment of a letter to Aristandros.

title: Letter from Demeas to Zenon
summary: Letter from Demeas (?) to Zenon

title: Letter from Diokles to Zenon
summary: Zenon is requested to write to Phanias, the secretary of the cavalrymen, by Diokles (?).

title: Letter from Diokles to Zenon
summary: On behalf of an Egyptian called Paris, who had been selected for service in the native soldiery by Axapes the royal scribe of the Oxyrhynchite nome and had consequently run away.  Zenon is requested to write to an official called Themistokles and also to Petosiris, another of the royal scribes.

title: Letter from Dioskourides to Zenon
summary: Dioskourides, a farmer employed by Zenon, informs him that a peasant in Pharbaithos offers to sell him three female asses, but that, if he wants to buy them, he must do so at once.  He also asks him to send a man to Akanthopolis to procure hay, for otherwise they will be obliged to buy in the market at a high price.

title: Letter from Petosiris to Zenon
summary: A request to Zenon to order payment to be made for the bricks supplied for the construction of granaries and other works.  Written in duplicate.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: After transacting some business concerning certain vineyards in the neighbourhood of Memphis, the writer had postponed his journey up the river, as he wished to pay his respects to Diotimos.  But being informed that Zenon wished to see him, he had arranged to sail up to Kerke and ride across to Philadelphia, when he heard that Zenon was coming to sacrifice at the Hellenion in Memphis. Through some mistake they did not meet, and he now asks Zenon to write and let him know if the business is urgent. He also begs for 200 drachmae (it appears that Zenon had some money of his in hand) in order to pay the taxes on the vineyards and recover the securities which he had deposited.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter, chiefly about an improper delivery of othonia.

title: Letter to Zenon
summary: A letter asking Zenon to pay Diotimos two hundred drachmae for a calf which he had bought for the writer in Hermopolis.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a report, probably by an agent of Zenon, about various inspections and inquiries which he had been making.

title: Division of Inherited Property
summary: The parties to the agreement are Chaeremon, son of Ptolemaeus and Ta�sis, and his nieces Ta�sion and Kyrillous, daughters of Chaeremon's deceased brother Kopres.  The latter, since they are minors, are represented in this transaction by their maternal uncle Ammonius, who is also their guardian. When Kopres died in 283/4, probably intestate, his brother Chaeremon took possession of two arouras of grain land and cultivated them for the following thirteen years with full responsibility for all taxes and other dues. Now in 296, in response to demands made by his nieces, he surrenders to them both arouras with the right to hold and cultivate them on the same terms for another thirteen years. At the conclusion of this period, i.e. in 309/310 A.D., Chaeremon will take one of the arouras and his nieces the other.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter to Zenon.

title: Part of a letter to Zenon
summary: The writer had apparently been commissioned to procure a thick cloak, and some flock, but had failed to find either.  He goes on to ask Zenon to send money for the conveyance of the vetch crop to the place of embarkation and to get a letter from Hermaphilos the oikonomos licensing the export.

title: Part of a letter to Zenon
summary: The writer had been asked to return three mattocks which he had borrowed from Doxaios.  He replies that Doxaios had only given him two, one of which was broken and required to be mended, and that he has now sent them back.  For confirmation of his statement he refers to Eutychos the door-keeper.

title: Letter to Zenon
summary: A petition from a man whose wife seems to have been taken to prison during his absence, begging Zenon to have her released.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter to Zenon, about preparations for a festival.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: Mnesitheos was proposing to lecture about Homer at Philadelphia and Zenon is requested by his correspondent to collect as many pupils as possible.

title: Letter to Zenon
summary: The writer, probably a cleruch, speaks of a piece of land which Zenon has leased from him and planted with vines.  He states that he has agreed to give half the rent to his sons, or to the sons of some other person, for their maintenance, and he asks Zenon to pay them their share directly, after deducting from the rent the amounts due for various taxes.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter to Zenon.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter to Zenon asking him to see that a slave is given up or three hundred drachmae paid as an equivalent.

title: Letter to Zenon
summary: A letter to Zenon, asking him to pay some money to Government in respect of a vineyard.

title: Division of Inherited Property
summary: There are five parties to this agreement: the daughters of Kopres, Ta�sis and Allous (Kyrillous, as she is called elsewhere); two brothers of Kopres, Castor and Chaeremon; a sister of Kopres, Clementina. The subject of the agreement is the division of two mortars among the five parties. One is given to Clementina and Castor, the other to Chaeremon, Ta�sis, and Allous. Althought the latter is in Chaeremon's possession, a half interest is assigned to Ta�sis and Allous.

title: Fragment of a letter and fragment of account
summary: The letter seems to have been written by one of Zenon's agricultural foremen.  On the other side of the papyrus is an account of daily expenses.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A fragment of a letter with a postscript, concerning the purchase of garments and other articles.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: A fragment of a letter from certain Greeks, who had been sent to work in or take charge of some vineyards, asking to be released from part of their task or to have more guards given to them.  The last lines show that it was sometimes difficult to make the

title: Letter to Zenon
summary: A letter to Zenon from a potter, who acknowledges receipt of some money and states what progress he has made with a quantity of jars which Zenon had sent down to be coated with pitch.

title: Letter to Prolemaios
summary: A note, possibly from Zenon, asking Ptolemaios to hire donkeys for transporting the figs, or possibly the sycamore wood.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter to Zenon, informing him that a slave,  probably belonging to the writer, had run away and had stolen certain articles.  A description of him is added below.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter to Zenon.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: A fragment of a letter to Zenon, about delivering a quantity of wine from the Philadelphian vineyards and about a payment to the oil monopoly.

title: Fragment of a letter to Zenon
summary: Announcing the despatch of a quantity of fish of various sorts.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: Probably part of a letter from a strategos to the village authorities, forwarding a petition and requesting them to reconcile the disputants or, if they cannot, to send them to him immediately.

title: Contract of Surety
summary: Greatly damaged contract of surety, probably involving Pancratius, son of Ptolemaeus and brother of Isidorus.

title: Fragment of a petition
summary: A fragment of a petition, probably addressed to the king and presented to the strategos, who then forwarded it to Zenon as head of the village.

title: Fragment of a petition to the king
summary: The petition had been forwarded to Zenon, probably with a request to send up the defendant for trial.

title: Draft of a petition to the king
summary: The subject of the dispute was the ownership of a house at present occupied by a woman called Arsinoe, who claimed to have built it on an empty site.  Zenon on the other hand claimed that the house was his and that it had been built by his servant Eutychos. But as Eutychos had formerly lived with Arsinoe and had children by her, she was able to persuade him, in order to avoid bearing witness in Zenon's favour, to take refuge in the temple of Sarapis at Memphis, where she kept him supplied with food. She then presented a petition to Agenor the strategos to the effect that Zenon had declared that he would produce as witness the men who built the house and supplied the materials, knowing that in the absence of Eutychos he would be unable to obtain their testimony. Agenor thereupon ordered Zenon to produce his witnesses or leave Arsinoe in occupation. Zenon now draws up a petition to the king, explaining how Eutychos had been got out of the way and asking that Arsinoe should be called upon to prove her claim and that, if she fails to do so, the house should be adjudged to him.

title: Draft of a petition to the king
summary: This draft deals with the same subject as PCZ.IV.59620, being in fact another draft of the same petition.  At the end the writer asks the king to order Agenor to examine the case and, if Arsinoe is unable to produce witnesses to show that she built the house, to decide in favour of the petitioner. But he has modified this sentence in a long interpolation or correction, in which he asks that, as he too is without witnesses, the strategos should hear the case anew and decide who seems right. The position at present was that Agenor had ordered Zenon to produce his witnesses or else leave Arsinoe in possession; and Zenon protests that, though the defection of Eutychos had deprived him of some essential evidence, it was not fair that Arsinoe should gain her suit without being called upon to show proof. In both drafts stress is laid upon Arsinoe's assertion, contradicted by Zenon, that he had definitely undertaken to produce the builders as witnesses.

title: Fragment of a petition to Zenon from Nikias
summary: The details of the petition are obscure, but apparently the king decided a case in which Nikias was involved and issued an order on the subject.

title: Fragment of a petition
summary: A fragment of a petition, probably addressed to Zenon.  The petitioner complains of being wronged by Hedylos.  He had been taken to prison and from there appeals to Zenon to make an inquiry and procure his liberation.

title: Part of a letter or memorandum to Zenon
summary: Part of a letter or memorandum about an aggressive person who had taken possession of a vineyard and a stock of wine.  Zenon is urged to use his influence with Diotimos in order that the trespasser may be brought before the chr�matistai.

title: Fragment of a petition
summary: The fragment as it stands is chiefly remarkable for the illiteracy of the scribe.  Apparently a complaint about the loss of certain utensils in connection with the selling of a slave.

title: Petition from Kallisthenes
summary: Kallisthenes had been arrested for failing to pay the balance of a debt, the creditor probably being Zenon.  He had given some of his goods in pledge and he possessed some wine still unsold; all this was likely to be lost if he was kept in prison.  He therefore begs Zenon, after summoning him and hearing his case, to set him free so that he may be able to collect the remainder of the money and pay his debt.

title: Fragment of a petition from Inaros
summary: The subject of the complaint is not clear, but the text contains a reference to the arrival or departure of certain the�roi.

title: Document concerning the lease of a mule
summary: Cursive.

title: Receipt for Rent
summary: An acknowledgment from a certain Ptolemaeus, whose further description is illegible, to Ptolemaeus, father of Isidorus, that he has received his share of the harvest from land which he possesses at Ptolemais Nea.  This was leased and cultivated in accordance with an agreement by which the lessor and the lessee divided the crop between them.

title: Petition to Zenon
summary: Petition to Zenon from a herdsman who seems to have been in trouble about his sheep and about the provision of green fodder, the price of which had been raised from four to six drachmae per aroura.  He begs Zenon to arrange matters so that he will not be imprisoned for debt.

title: Petition to Zenon
summary: A complaint from two natives who were probably farming a kl�ros and growing a crop of castor oil for Zenon.  There was some dispute between them and Horos the foreman about the area to be used for this purpose, and they appealed to Zenon to do them justice.

title: Fragment of a letter or memorandum
summary: Apparently a complaint about a dyer who had been charging too much for his work and a proposal of a new arrangement.

title: Letter
summary: A complaint about the lack of consideration with which the writer has been treated.  He mentions among other things that Zenon's cattle have grazed on his corn and that his servants have been beaten for trying to drive them off.

title: Complaint presented by Pyron
summary: A complaint with a formal heading, probably addressed to Apollonios.

title: Fragment of a report
summary: A fragment of a report.

title: Part of a letter
summary: Part of a letter, probably from one of Zenon's employees, reporting about the progress of various affairs.

title: Fragment of a letter or memorandum
summary: The writer intercedes on behalf of an arrested farmer and offers to guarantee that he will not decamp, if released.

title: Fragment of a letter or memorandum
summary: Possibly a draft or an unsent letter by Zenon himelf.

title: Fragment of a letter or memorandum
summary: A request, probably to Zenon, to intervene on behalf of a woman from whom a debt was being exacted by one of his agents, in order that the price of certain articles should be returned to her and that she should be enabled to pay the dues which she owed to the Crown.

title: Receipt for Rent
summary: An acknowledgment of payment of rent by Ptolemaeus, father of Isidorus, as tenant and cultivator of one aroura of land over a period of four years at the rate of two artabas of wheat per year.

title: Fragment of a petition
summary: A fragment of a petition from an Egyptian who seems to have been imprisoned for failing to execute a contract.

title: Fragment of a letter or memorandum
summary: Probably a request to send to the writer a labourer who had decamped before finishing the work which he had contracted to do.

title: Fragment of a letter or memorandum
summary: A fragment of a letter or memorandum.

title: Fragment of a letter or memorandum
summary: The writer had begun to build on a site supposed to belong to Zenon, to whom his complaint was probably addressed, but had been stopped by a certain Koroibides who claimed to be the owner of the ground.

title: Fragment of a letter or memorandum
summary: A fragment of a letter or memorandum to Zenon.

title: Fragment of a letter or memorandum
summary: A fragment of a letter or memorandum.

title: Fragment of a letter to Epharmostos
summary: A fragment of a letter to Epharmostos.

title: Memorandum
summary: A petition from a head clerk.  He wishes to have an office built for him near Zenon's, to be provided with a sufficient quantity of papyrus, a hundred rolls to begin with, and to have a definite arrangement about the salaries of himself and the three clerks who worked under him. From the figures given in column 3 it appears that an ordinary clerk earned little more than an unskilled labourer.

title: Fragment of a letter or memorandum
summary: A fragment of a letter or memorandum about boat-building and payment of the workmen.  They seem to have been repairing a boat and to have been about to build a small boat for towing astern.

title: Memorandum to Zenon
summary: A memorandum about a boat which apparently belonged to Zenon.  In lines 2 - 7 the writer states the present cost of upkeep.  In lines 8 - 14 he suggests an alternative way of paying the crew, by giving them monthly rations of wheat and deducting so much from their wages. In lines 15 - 19 he makes another proposal: he will pay Zenon a yearly rent of 500 drachmae; Zenon on the other hand will pay the Government tax, and if he requires the boat at any time, so much will be deducted from what the writer pays in rent and wages. Lines 20 - 28 propose still another arrangement: instead of paying rent the writer will be responsible for the Government tax as well as the sailors' wages, and the boat will be at Zenon's service when required, the only burden on Zenon being the provision of 72 artabs of wheat for the sailors and 18 for the writer. But naturally Zenon will bear the cost of the necessary repairs.

title: Receipt for Rent
summary: An acknowledgment of payment of rent for the third year of Probus on land which lay partly in Karanis and partly in Ptolemais Nea.  Issued by Aphrodisius, a soldier, to Ptolemaeus, father of Isidorus.

title: Memorandum to Zenon from Harnouthis
summary: A memorandum to Zenon from Harnouthis, about a boat in which Zenon had a financial interest.

title: Memorandum to Zenon from Nikarchos
summary: The writer complains that his farming partner Dionysodoros is not paying his share of the expenses.  He asks Zenon to compel him to pay at once the amount which he admittedly owes and to examine him (?) about the sum which he disputes.

title: Memorandum about swineherds
summary: A memorandum about swineherds.

title: Memorandum
summary: A request to some person sailing down to Alexandria to deliver a letter at Hermopolis to a certain Orilaos.

title: Memorandum to Zenon
summary: A memorandum to Zenon from an employee or dependent asking for the corn allowance, oil allowance and salary which he has not received for more than a year.

title: Memorandum to Zenon and another person
summary: An account of money owed by certain persons, possibly from Petosiris.

title: Memorandum
summary: A request for a loan of croton and sesame seed from one of the cavalrymen in Herakleia.  The names on the verso were probably written by Zenon with reference to some other matter.

title: Memorandum to Zenon
summary: A memorandum to Zenon.

title: Part of a memorandum
summary: Part of a memorandum.

title: Notification of a robbery
summary: The writer announces that, when travelling to Philadelphia with two companions, he fell in with robbers who bound him and took from him two donkeys belonging to Zenon, all his clothing, and everything that he had down to his signet ring and his walking stick. The stolen articles are enumerated and valued, the total valuation amounting to 225 drachmae, 2 1/2 obols.

title: Receipt for Rent
summary: Undated acknowledgment of payment of rent for the fifth year, presumably in the reign of Probus, on two arouras in the village of Karanis.  Isued by Aphrodisius, a soldier, to Ptolemaeus, father of Isidorus.

title: Note about payment of tax
summary: A request for payment of the import tax on the wine which Paboys had brought from the Aphroditopolite nome.

title: Note about the produce of the wine-vats
summary: The writer, probably a wine-maker, complains that the quantities of wine reported by Gorgilos to have been obtained from three of the vats are considerably greater than the quantities actually obtained.

title: Estimate concerning bronze-casting (?)

title: Account of daily expenses
summary: An account of daily expenses during a cruise.  At the top is a list of the nomes between Memphis and Hermopolis; then comes an account of three days' expenses; and below is a further list of provisions, the cost of which is to be divided among four people probably the crew of the boat. On the margin and on the verso are some slight sketches of human figures and other subjects. The best of them represents a man or an Apollo holding a lyre of fifteen strings, which ends below in animals' heads and has a loop round one of its sides.

title: Fragment of account of household wheat
summary: A fragment of an account of household wheat.  An interesting detail is the provision made for guests invited to dinner on the occasion of the festival which commemorated the assumption of kingship by the Ptolemaic dynasty.

title: Fragment of account of household wheat

title: Account of barley
summary: An account of barley given out for the feeding of pigs, donkeys, horses, dogs and pigeons, the measure employed being the an�l�tikon, in which the artab contained forty choinikes.  Two of the pigs were Sicilian; this was a famous breed in antiquity, and no doubt the presence of these imported specimens at Philadelphia was due to Apollonios. Column 5 shows us that the dogs in Zenon's charge were well cared for.

title: Account of barley
summary: An account of barley.  Probably part of the same document as PCZ.IV.59710, though the two pieces do not fit together.

title: Report to a Strategos
summary: A report from Aurelius Sarapion, an asistant in the office of the strategos Aurelius Heron, to his principal.  From Isid.65 we know that Isidorus, son of Ptolemaeus, filed a request with the strategos on August 20th, 298 A.D., that his assistant be sent to Karanis to inspect certain crops which had been destroyed by fire. Isidorus suspected arson and wished to have his complaint certified even though the culprits had not yet been identified. In compliance with his request, Sarapion was sent to Karanis, and he now informs the strategos that the facts are as stated by Isidorus.

title: Account of barley
summary: An account of the quantities of barley given daily to the dogs and to a certain Myrmes.

title: Heading of document about corn

title: Fragment of corn account
summary: A fragment of a corn account.  The entries, some of which are checked by slanting strokes and others by two cross strokes in the right margin, state the quantities of olyra and other grain that have been lent to certain persons, presumably for seed.  The loan is in some cases repayable in kind at the rate of 150 percent; in other cases the borrower has the option of repaying in kind at the said rate or of paying the price of the borrowed grain in money. An exception is made in favour of a donkey-driver, who is not required, if he elects to repay in kind, to return more than the amount which he borrowed.

title: Account of deliveries of grain
summary: An account of deliveries of grain.  The account was kept by the Macedonian months and the grain was carried by mules.

title: Account of sesame and other crops
summary: An account of sesame, poppy seed and croton which had been disposed of and states the quantities which still remain.

title: Account of straw or hay
summary: An account; the material is not specified, but it was reckoned by loads and sheaves, twenty of the latter being equal to one of the former.  As it seems to have been used as fuel for baths and as binding mud bricks, as well as forming food for donkeys and sheep, it was probably straw rather than hay.

title: Letter to a Praepositus Pagi
summary: Heraclides, praepositus of the fifth pagus of the Arsinoite nome, writes to the praepositus of another pagus, reminding him of an imperial constitution which requires all strangers found to be residing in the villages, to be handed over to the fiscus, and establishes a reward of five folles for each person so surrendered. Since the village of Karanis has complained to Heraclides that some of its villagers are now in the pagus administered by his colleague, he requests the latter to compel the villages subject to his authority to give up any fugitives who are shown to belong to Karanis. In conclusion, Heraclides offers to cooperate in carrying out the terms of the law by returning any fugitives from his colleagues's pagus who may be found in his own.

title: Fragment of stable account
summary: A fragment of a stable account.

title: Account concerning hay-land
summary: Part of a large document dealing with corn-land as well as hay-land.  The section concerning the hay-crop has been cut off, and above the first line are vestiges of figures belonging to the preceding section.  Out of a parcel of 1,355 3/4 arouras 1000 had been reserved for the Crown according to the terms of the lease, i.e. their estimated produce amounting to 1,200,000 sheaves was to be delivered to the Crown at the fixed price of 66 2/3 drachmae per 10,000 sheaves. As for the remaining 355 3/4 arouras, the farmers were free to sell the hay privately, if they could thus obtain a better pricee, but the money received would have to be deposited in the bank until they had bought sufficient barley to pay their rent in kind; if however they preferred to deliver the crop to the Crown, the Crown would accept it at the lower rate of 58 1/3 drachmae per 1000 sheaves. The final paragraph adds that it has been agreed that the prices payable by the Crown shall be increased by one half in the case of the 1000 arouras and by five sevenths in the case of the 355 3/4, so that in both cases the rate would become 100 drachmae.

title: Account between landlord and lessee
summary: An account between landlord and lessee.  The landlord, who speaks as if he were a cleruch with a holding of 100 arouras, had leased his holding at a rent of two artabs of wheat for each aroura and had also provided the lessee with 37 1/2 artabs for seed.

Lines 15-29 report that the Egyptian Pasis has given Andronikos, the agent despatching the loads, eight drachmae not to allow Dionysodoros to cut any vine-shoots or rose-shoots, as owing to his ignorance he was damaging the vines.

title: Claim for transport of wine

In return he was given this quittance against any additional claim upon him or his village.

title: Apollonius, Homeric lexicon
summary: Of the codex, which must have contained the entire Homeric lexicon of Apollonius in approximately 40 double pages, only half of a double page and another small fragment remain. The lemmata of this lexicon are in strict alphabetical order.

title: Letter of Euphronius to Milon
summary: Letter to Milon to ask him to have a list of payments and grain deliveries made by the trapezitai and thesauros-officials of the temples.

title: Letter of Euphronius to Milon
summary: An official letter concerning the arrest of certain transgressors.

title: Letter of Andron to Milon
summary: Private letter of Andron to his brother Milon.  From P.Eleph.10 we know that Andron and Milon also had an official connection, and the former was perhaps a trapezit�s in a temple bank.

title: Offer of Phatres
summary: An offer by a man called Phatres concerning houses and other possessions of Psentaes.

title: Lease of Land and Receipt for Rent
