title: Petition of a naucleros
summary: Libys the naukl�ros was held back by an accident and did not make it to the Thebaid, where he was to load his boat with wheat, which he was charged to carry for the fiscus. Now he asks to be allowed to load his ship with wheat from the Arsinoite Nome, which is closest. He ends by swearing a royal oath. The verso contains a summary of the petition.

title: Incomplete delivery of an order for wine
summary: Three dealers, Sopatros, Dionysios and Ptolemaios, complain against a wine merchant, Petenenteris, who has delivered to them an incomplete order for wine. The verso contains a summary of the petition.

title: Reimbursement of wine
summary: Diocles is complaining about Seos, who is has not yet repaid to him the 30 measures of wine that he owes him, even though the matter had been already taken once to the epistates. So Diocles asks that Seos be forced to repay to the epistates on his account he value of the wine, in kind or money. The verso contains a summary of the petition.

title: Payment for fodder
summary: Heracleides demands the 5 drachmas that Aristomachos owes him. The verso contains a summary of the petition.

title: Loan of a donkey
summary: Theodotos demands the return of a donkey he lent several months previously to Nikias,  who is obstinately not returning it. The verso contains a summary of the petition.

title: Loan of money
summary: Dioscourides and Nicanor complain about Nephorsouchis, who borrowed money from them and then moved without returning it. The verso contains a summary of the petition.

title: Return of a contract
summary: Pechysios owes money to Neandros, and there is a contract which proves this. The contract had been given to a certain Pet[  ] for safe keeping, but he, in the meantime, has died, and the contract is now in the hands of his son Paos, who is refusing to return it. Neandros demands it back. The verso contains a summary of the petition.

title: Validity of an unsealed contract
summary: Petoys complains about Lachares and Alexandros, who refuse to accept as valid a contract for the lease of land. They claim that the contract was deposited while still unsealed, and therefore they are not bound to repay Petoys for letting his land. verso contains a summary of the petition.

title: Execution of the clauses of a lease
summary: Theodotos, Gaddaios and Phanias complain about Demetrios, who, despite the contract which they concluded, refused to come and witness the insufficient inundation of the land that they rented from him. The verso contains a summary of the petition.

title: Trespassing of land
summary: Hediste, a Macedonian, complains that Demetrios the doctor wants to build on a plot of land that belongs to her and asks that the strategos stop him immediately. The verso contains a summary of the petition.

title: Private Account
summary: Private account.  About half the lines are fairly legible.

title: Violence against a steward
summary: Crateuas, a Macedonian steward of cleroi, was attacked by some shepherds, because he was accusing them of pasturing their flock unauthorised on a plot of land which was under his supervision.

title: Homer, Iliad xxi
summary: The upper part of a column, no doubt the second of the roll, containing ll. 26-41 of Homer, Iliad xxi.

title: Petition of Demetrius
summary: Petition addressed to Cleopatra III and Ptolemy Soter II by Demetrius, an infantry soldier domiciled at Euhemeria, concerning the repayment of certain loans of wheat lent by him to Theotimus, son of Phileas.

title: Banker's receipts
summary: Three receipts for sums paid into the royal bank at Crocodilopolis by Marres and two women, Tamarres and Hierobasis (?), respectively.

title: Proclamation of Mamertinus
summary: Proclamation of the praefect Marcus Petronius Mamertinus, ordering receipts to be given for payments made.

title: List of persons qualified for office
summary: This papyrus, of which parts of three columns are preserved, is a list of persons with the offices which they had held or were holding and the value of their property.

title: Declaration concerning an Edict
summary: Declaration on oath addressed to Diodorus, strategos of the divisions of Themistes and Polemon, by a village police officer, that he had put up at a certain farmstead a copy of an edict of the praefect Sempronius Liberalis ordering persons who were staying away from their homes to return to them.

title: Official correspondence
summary: A reply from the scribes of the metropolis and the village scribes of the divisions of Themistes and Polemon to a letter sent to them by the strategus.

title: Delegation of Tax-Collecting
summary: Agreement between Heron, an inhabitant of Philagris, and two tax assistants, by which Heron undertakes to act as a substitute for them, and to collect certain taxes on domain (?) land at the village of Polydeucia, cultivated by inhabitants of Philagris.

title: Receipt for salary of Deputy Tax-Collectors
summary: Acknowledgement by two persons to a tax-collector of Theadelpheia that they had received from him the sum of 200 drachmae as their 'salary' for acting as his deputy during one year.

title: Receipt for tim� purou
summary: Tax receipt.

title: Lease of a Monopoly
summary: An undertaking on the part of a certain Sanesneus to pay 80 drachmae, with various extra sums, to the 'superintendents of brick-making in the nome', in consideration of his having been granted for one year the right to make and sell bricks at the village of Kerketho�ris, with power to hand on his rights to others.

title: Warrant for Arrest
summary: Order addressed to the archephodos of Psenuris, probably by a military officer, for the arrest of a person called Emes.

title: Report to a Tax-Farmer
summary: Reply from the elders of Theadelpheia to the farmer of a tax connected with the temples at the village of Boukol�n, stating, in answer to a question addressed to them by the tax-farmer, who was the person responsible for a certain payment in relation to the tax.

title: Receipt for Tax on Beer
summary: The tax upon beer, which was a regular impost in both the Ptolemaic and Roman periods, is the subject of this receipt and P.Fay.047A, which acknowledge a number of payments on this account from a certain Petesuchus, amounting in the one case to 8 drachmae, in the other, probably referring to the following year, to 12 drachmae.

title: Receipt for Tax on Beer
summary: A receipt for the payment by Onnophris of ten drachmae two and a half obols on account of beer-tax, and for another payment, probably the same amount, on behalf of another person, for the same tax.

title: Receipts for Tax on Weaving
summary: Two receipts, the first for five payments amounting to 31 drachmae, on account of the gerdiakon or tax on weavers, the second probably referring to a sixth payment for the same tax.

title: Receipt for Poll-Tax
summary: This and the three following papyri are receipts for payments of poll-tax.  The amount of this tax varied considerably at different districts and at different periods; and there were also distinct rates corresponding to distinctions of status.

title: Receipt for Poll-Tax
summary: Receipt for the payment by Chaeras of 20 drachmae, being the poll-tax for the 22nd year of Commodus.

title: Receipt for Poll-Tax
summary: Receipt for the payment by two brothers of poll-tax and epistatikou hiere�n the total sum paid amounting to 16 drachmae, no doubt payment on account.

title: Receipt for Poll-Tax
summary: Receipt for 20 drachmae paid for poll-tax by Souchammon, son of Castor.

title: Contract
summary: Contract agreed by the koinon of Aphrodito with the poimenes and the agrophulakoi (sic) of the village, governing their conditions of work. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipt for Poll-Tax
summary: Receipt for two payments of eight drachmae on account for poll-tax by a slave.

title: Tax-Receipt
summary: This receipt is of a miscellaneous character, acknowledging payments from a certain Onnophris on account of no less than seven different taxes.

title: Tax-Receipt
summary: A receipt for naubion kat(oik�n) and minor taxes connected with it all paid in copper obols, followed by an entry of a payment in silver 'for kat(oikoi).'

title: Tax-Receipt
summary: A receipt for naubion kat(oik�n) and minor taxes like P.Fay.056

title: Payment for Use of Pastures
summary: Acknowledging the receipt of 48 drachmae huper phor(ou) nom�n.

title: Receipt for Tax on the Sale of a Cow
summary: A receipt for payment of the tax on a cow, which had lately been bought for 44 drachmae.

title: List of Fines
summary: A list of payments by various persons for fines officially imposed apparently as the result of an inquiry.

title: Custom-House Receipt
summary: One of a large number of similar receipts relating to taxes paid at the custom-house of the outlying villages in the Fayum by persons crossing the desert to Memphis or the oases.

title: Custom-House Receipt
summary: One of a large number of similar receipts relating to taxes paid at the custom-house of the outlying villages in the Fayum by persons crossing the desert to Memphis or the oases.

title: Custom-House Receipt
summary: One of a large number of similar receipts relating to taxes paid at the custom-house of the outlying villages in the Fayum by persons crossing the desert to Memphis or the oases.

title: Petition
summary: Petition of the inhabitants of Aphrodito to Fl. Marianos, against the actions of Menas, pagarch of Antaiopolis, and his employees. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Custom-House Receipt
summary: One of a large number of similar receipts relating to taxes paid at the custom-house of the outlying villages in the Fayum by persons crossing the desert to Memphis or the oases.

title: Work on the Embankments
summary: Certificate for labour carried out on the maintenance of canals and embankments

title: Receipt for Payment in Kind
summary: Acknowledgement by the sitologoi of Berenicis-on-shore of the payment of 74 23/24 artabae of wheat and 38 2/3 artabae of barley from the kl�rouchoi of Berenicis and others.

title: Receipt for Payment in Kind
summary: Acknowledgement by the sitologoi of Euhemeria of a payment of 4 9/24 artabae of wheat 'for the katoikoi of Euhemeria' to the account of Sarapias, daughter of Sarapion.

title: Receipt for Payment in Kind
summary: Acknowledgement by the sitologoi of Nestou epoik(ion), a village near Bacchias, of the payment of 1 1/6 artabae of wheat 'for the katoikoi of Hephaestias'.  The payment is made by Petesiris, probably a ge�rgos, to the account of Kopes (?).

title: Receipt for Payment in Kind
summary: Acknowledgement by the dekapr�toi, who in the third century take the place of the sitologoi in this class of receipts, of 60 3/4 artabae of wheat and 6 3/4 artabae of barley huper d�mosi�n, and 3 artabae huper katoik�n, paid by Patereus.

title: Loan of Seed
summary: Acknowledgement of a loan of vegetable-seed, to be repaid in the following month.  No interest was required, but the lender was to have the choice of accepting either the seed or its value in money, estimated to the highest current price.

title: Contract for Labour in an Oil-Press
summary: A contract for the engagement of a woman named Thenetkoueis to serve for the season in an oil-press belonging to Lucius Bellenus Gemellus at a daily wage, the exact amount of which is not stated, but of which she receives an advance of 16 drachmae.

title: Lease of an Oil-Press
summary: Proposal addressed to Achilleus, through his guardian Sarapion, by Apollonius for the lease of an oil-press at Dionysias for four years at the rent of one metretes six choes of both olive and raphanus oil with various extra payments.

title: Receipt for the Rent of an Oil-Press
summary: A notice issued through the bank of Sarapion, probably at Arsino�, by Syrus to Nemesas, stating that he had paid Nemesas 5 metretae of oil, being the year's rent of an oil-press belonging to Pompeius Ptolemaeus, a gymnasiarch, of whose property Nemesas was acting as guardian.

title: Petition
summary: Petition addressed to Fl. Theodorus by the monks of the convent of the Christ-bearing Apostles of Pharaous, about a plot of 6 arouras of land, which a widow has left them. A certain Ezechiel contests their ownership. On the verso the subscription reads: an entreaty and prayers of your most pitiable servants... Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipt for a Share of an Inheritance
summary: Receipt for 20 drachmae paid to Maron as his share of an inheritance from his deceased father, by his younger brother Onnophris.

title: Receipt for House-Rent
summary: Acknowledgement by Heraclides and Nilus of the receipt from Tauris of a year's rent for a house at Euhemeria which was leased by her to them.

title: Receipt for Farm-Rent
summary: Acknowledgement by Didyme of the receipt of 2 1/4 artabae (of wheat?) from her tenant Heron.

title: Order on a Bank
summary: An order addressed to a banker by a woman called Aphrodous requesting him to pay two women both names Charition six hundred drachmae, being the price of half a house and appurtenances at Theadelpheia, bought by her from them.  At the end are acknowledgements of the receipt of the money by the two vendors, written by their respective husbands.

title: Farm Accounts
summary: Of this papyrus, which originally may have been a roll of some length, the last column and the ends of lines of the column preceding it are preserved, with two detached fragments from an earlier column.  The last two columns are occupied with accounts of wages paid on different days for agricultural labour.

title: Account
summary: Parts of two columns of an account, which is unfortuntely broken in such a manner that only the figures of the first column and the  names of the second remain.  The largeness of the sums in the first column indicates the late date of the papyrus.  The payments in the second column are for a variety of purposes, but a number of them are connected with a boat or boats, and perhaps they may all have been made on the occasion of a journey by water. Nicopolis is mentioned in line 12.

title: Petition of Papontos
summary: A petition complaining of the theft of various skins and fleeces, and asking for redress.

title: Petition to the Strategus
summary: A petition addressed to Megalonymus, strategus of the divisions of Themistes and Polemon, by Pasion and Onesimus, two 'pig-merchants' at Arsino�, complaining of a robbery with violence committed upon them as they were journeying home from Theadelpheia.

title: Letter of Pisais
summary: A letter from Pisais to Heracleus asking him to pay 12 drachmae to Cleon.

title: Letter from Gemellus to Sabinus
summary: This letter is almost identical to another written a few days earlier, both containing directions that a man should be sent to see an olive-yard belonging to a friend called Hermonax, which required thinning.  Probably Sabinus did not immediately comply, and Gemellus became impatient and wrote again. At the end of each letter is a request for fish for certain festal occasions.

title: Petition
summary: Petition addressed to the dux Fl. Marianos from a widow called Sophia.  Her first husband died leaving her a young child, and she has been unjustly divested of what he left to her.  Marrying a second time, a certain Senouthes has caused the death of her econd husband, and taken to committing acts of violence against her. Finally, a man called Jeremiah, boethos from the village of Aphrodito, has taken her child from her, and refuses to give it back. Subscription in four lines on the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter from Sabinus to Geminus
summary: This letter and P.Fay.122 (to Epagathus) were written by Sabinus, the son of Gemellus.  Since Sabinus addresses them both in the same way his relationship was perhaps the same to them both.

title: Letter from Sabinus to Epagathus
summary: This letter and P.Fay.121 (to Geminus) were written by Sabinus, the son of Gemellus.  Since Sabinus addresses them both in the same way his relationship was perhaps the same to them both.

title: Letter from Harpocration to Sabinus
summary: This letter is addressed to Sabinus, the son of Gemellus, by a brother named Harpocration, of whom we have not before heard.  The  most interesting point in it is a mention of a Jew called Teuphilus (Theophilus), who had apparently been chosen as a cultivator of the domain-lands, and wished to be released from this service.

title: Letter from Theogiton to Apollonius
summary: This letter was found with the Gemellus papyri, but the persons concerned were not so far as we know members of the family.  The letter is a strong remonstrance addressed by a relative or friend to a man who was defrauding his mother of some allowance (chor�gia), and threatens prosecution if this conduct was persisted in.

title: Letter of a Chief Priest
summary: Letter from Ptolemaeus, archiereus, to his brother Heron, urging him to use all his efforts to become successful in an election to some office, perhaps that of strategos. The second part of the letter continues with an obscure reference to some allowance that would be made by the writer to his brother in connexion with an embl�ma.

title: Letter to a Father
summary: Letter of Dioxenus to his father Sarapion, asking him to return home in order to attend to the fixing of boundaries of a piece of land.

title: Letter of a Daughter
summary: A letter from Taorsenouphis to her mother, requesting the delivery of some grapes to the sister of the writer's mother and announcing the dispatch of various articles.

title: Letter of Midas
summary: A letter from Midas to Akous, perhaps the writer's son, asking to to tell Posidonius, who had referred Midas to a certain Ponticus in connexion with the sale or lease of a house, of Ponticus' refusal to negotiate.

title: Letter to Serenus
summary: A short letter to Serenus from some person informing him that his presence was wanted.

title: Letter of Mysthes
summary: Letter from Mysthes to his brother Serapammon, saying tht he was looking after some copper (money?) until he met Serapammon at a festival.

title: Petition
summary: Petition from Maria, a widow from the village of Sabbis in the Theodosiopolite nome, to the dux of the Thebaid (the name is missing, but according to the palaeography and date, probably Fl. Marianos).  The magistrates of the village want to impose on her

title: Letter to Sarapion
summary: A letter addressed to Sarapion by a person whose name is lost, giving him directions about the sale of some barley and the irrigation of a farm.

title: Letter of Alypius
summary: This letter, which is written on the verso of P.Fay.104 is from Alypius to Heron, giving some directions about making wine.

title: Letter of Eudaemon
summary: A letter from Eudaemon to Longinus asking him to come and bring the hualos, apparently here a stone implement of some kind for clipping coins, in order that the writer might get some wine with the proceeds of this (nefarious) transaction.

title: Christian Letter
summary: Concluding part of a letter in which the writer urges the addressees to return to their homes.

title: Horoscope
summary: The recto of this papyrus contains parts of two columns of an account of legal proceedings(?) in a very mutilated condition.  On the verso is the beginning of a horoscope, of which only the name of the person was written and the date, given as usual by the two calendars, the 'Greek' (i.e the Julian) and the 'ancient' (in which there was no leap year). The date in question is Mesore 5 = Thoth 16 in the first year of Marcus and Verus (July 29th, A.D. 161)

title: Homer, Iliad
summary: 14 fragments of two incomplete columns containing Homer, Iliad i 273-362.

title: Letter of Pnepheros
summary: Letter of Pnepheros (?) to his mother Tamizas (?).  Incomplete.
date_text: Late third or early fourth century

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt, written by Sambas hupodekt�s for 1 11/12 artabae of corn paid 'for the corn of the tenth indiction at Karanis'.  Cancelled.

title: Letter from Isidorus
summary: Letter from Isidorus, complete, but much obliterated.  Dated in Thoth of the twenth-eighth year of a Ptolemy.

title: Acknowledgement of Receipt
summary: Acknowledgement by Heraclides of the receipt of 24 1/3 artabae from Herais, being a year's rent.

title: Marriage contract
summary: Long fragment of a marriage contract, in very bad Greek, full of spelling errors and grammatical mistakes. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Homer, Iliad
summary: 10 fragments containing Homer, Iliad xx 36-110 in two much mutilated columns

title: Order
summary: Order to the archephodos of Bacchias to send up various accused persons

title: Acknowledgement of Receipt
summary: Acknowledgement by the sitologoi of Bacchias Hephaestias of the receipt of 3 2/3 artabae of wheat, and 1/24 artabae for prosmetroumena, for the k(at)oi(koi) of Hephaestias, paid to the credit of Secundus (?) dia Tesenouphe�s.

title: Account
summary: Account, complete as far as it goes, of receipts and payments.

title: Receipt for Tax
summary: Receipt for customs dues paid by Chairemon at Bakchias for exporting 12 eri�n pokous on a donkey.

title: Receipt for Poll-Tax
summary: Receipt for 20 drachmae paid by Heron for poll-tax of the fifteenth year, and  [10] obols of copper for prosdiagraphomena.

title: Receipt for Poll-Tax
summary: Receipt for 20 drachmae paid by Soterichus also called Horus for poll-tax of the sixth year, and 10 obols of copper for prosdiagraphomena.

title: Tax-Receipt
summary: Beginning of a tax-receipt dated in the thirteenth year of Aurel. Antonius Caesar the lord, Arm. Med. Parth. Germ. Max., Mecheir.

title: List of Persons
summary: List of persons.  Written in red ink.

title: List of Persons
summary: Parts of two columns of a list of persons, with amounts of arourae (?).  Dated in the twenty-second year of Imp. Caes. Marcus Aurel. Severus Anton. Parth. Max. Brit. Max. Germ. Max. Pius Aug.

title: Account
summary: Not the same hand as recto.

title: Fragment of a petition
summary: Fragment of a petition addressed to Fl. Marianos the dux of the Thebaid, from Maximinus, a monk from the monastery of Saint Jeremiah at Antaiou. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Account of Legal Proceedings
summary: Two fragments containing parts of 26 lines of an account of legal proceedings.

title: Collection of Maxims
summary: Beginnings of 12 lines of a collection of Maxims (?).

title: Contract of a Loan
summary: Ends of 13 lines of a contract for a loan of corn to be returned in Pauni.
date_text: Middle or end of second century

title: Return
summary: Conclusion of a return probably relating to the epikrisis of a boy.

title: Acknowledgement of Payment
summary: Acknowledgement by the sitologoi of Bacchias Hephaestias of a payment of wheat.

title: Homer, Iliadiii
summary: Fragment containing ends of Homer, Il. iii 214-224

title: Homer, Iliadviii
summary: Four fragments containing parts of Homer, Iliad viii 41-54, 86-104, 139-156, and 173-186 written in a medium-sized uncial hand, on the verso of some second century accounts.

title: Contract of a Loan
summary: Contract between Maron and Ninnas for the loan of 40 drachmae.

title: Acknowledgement of Receipt
summary: Acknowledgement by Apollonius of the receipt of rent for the twenty-eighth year from Horus and another.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for 140 drachmae paid at Euhemeria in the thirteenth year of Aurelius Antoninus the lord, Mesore.

title: Poetry
summary: Parts of 29 verses, almost entirely illegible. Abrasion has made the upper layer of the papyrus disappear. Only ll. 14 and 19 are reproduced. This piece has been written by the same author whose works we find in a certain number of the Maspero papyri, for we can recognise those two lines (c.f. P.Cair.Masp. I 35). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros. See Fournet, Hell�nisme (MIFAO 115) p. 429 no. IV 29

title: List of landed property
summary: On the recto part of two columns of a list of landed property.

title: Certificate of labour
summary: Certificate of work done at the embankments from Mesore 10-14 by Athenas.

title: Acknowledgement of Receipt
summary: Acknowledgement by six presbuteroi of Autodike of the receipt of 120 drachmae on account from the kt�notrophoi.

title: Letter
summary: Letter from [Ni]unaros to his son Zosimus, announcing a dispatch.

title: List of names
summary: List of names with amounts of money in three columns.  On the verso part of five columns of a similar list dated in the thirteenth year (of Augustus), Pachon.

title: Notice of a death
summary: Notice addressed to Didymus of the division of Themistes, by Sisois, announcing the death of his maternal uncle.

title: Acknowledgement of Return of Loan
summary: Acknowledgement by Char[es?] of the return of a loan of 200 drachmae from Soterichus.

title: Contract of a Loan
summary: Contract for a loan of three artabae of rafaninon to Achilles.  At the top are four lines giving an abstract of the loan, with numerous abbreviations, a blank space being left for the name of the lender.  The middle of the papyrus is blank.  At the bottom are the acknowledgement of the borrower and docket of the grapheion of Euhemeria.
date_text: Late second or first century B.C.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for 144 litrai Italikai of hay.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for telos mosch(ou) at Socnopaei Nesus, paid to a nomarch through Didymus logeut�s (?) , by Gaius Papirius Maximus.

title: Petition
summary: Petition addressed to Fl. Marianos, the dux of the Thebaid, from Ischyrion, son in law of Apollos, protocometes of the village of Aphrodito.  The content of the document is lost, just the opening formula is saved. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for 16 drachmae of ruparou arguriou eis l(ogon) laog(raphias) of the seventeenth year

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for 20 drachmae and 10 obols for prosdiagraphomena paid for laographia of the second year at the amphodon Mo�(re�s) by Polydeuces.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for 20 drachmae and 10 obols for prosdiagraphomena paid for laographia of the fourteenth year at the amphodon Dion(usiou) Top(�n) by Castor.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for 20 drachmae and 10 obols for prosdiagraphovmena paid for laographia of the third year of the amphodon Li(bos).

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for 20 drachmae and 10 obols for prosdiagraphomena paid for laographia

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for 20 drachmae and 10 obols for prosdiagraphomena paid for laographia of the fourteenth year at the amphodon Dion(usiou) Top(�n).

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for 20 drachmae and 10 obols for prosdiagraphomena paid for laographia of the twenty-first (?) year.

title: Certificate of labour
summary: Certificate of work done on the embankments at Euhemeria by Menches in the reign of Tib. Claudius Caes. Aug. Germ. Imp.

title: Certificate of labour
summary: Certificate for five days' work at the embankments en têi Fol(  ) diô(rugi) by Sathepas.

title: Certificate of labour
summary: Certificate for two days' work at the embankments.

title: Draft of petition
summary: A draft of a petition to the dux of the Thebaid. The inhabitants of Antaiopolis complain about a certain Florentios, a strategos.  The first time, he has stifled their complaints. Consequently, they decide to inform the dux once more of his abuses of power. The recto and the verso of the papyrus are used. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Certificate of labour
summary: Certificate for five days' work at the embankments en c�(mati?) Dru(  ).

title: Certificate of labour
summary: Certificate for five days' work at the embankments by Sisois.

title: List of Abstracts
summary: Two incomplete columns of a list of abstracts of contracts.

title: Account
summary: Part of an account of corn, beginning  eisin hai eisachtheisai.  The entries are made under the names of various people.

title: Contract for a sale
summary: Contract for the sale of two horses for apparently 440 drachmae, from Heraclides to a woman named Didarous.

title: Application
summary: Application from certain machairophoroi asking ean phain�tai suntaxin doth�nai h�min to metr�ma kai to ops�nion tou Th�uth kai Pha�phi andr�n pente.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for laographia headed antigraphon cheirogr(aphon) sumbolou. Hermias grammateus prak(torôn) k. t. l.

title: Receipt for Land-Rent
summary: Receipt for rent of land near Philoteris paid by Hermes, ge�rgos.

title: Homer, Iliad ii
summary: Parts of two columns containing the ends of Homer, Il. ii 611-646, and beginnings of 647-683.

title: Philosophical Treatise
summary: On the recto ends of 9 lines from some philosophical treatise.  On the verso parts of two columns of a similar work in the same (?) hand

title: Fragment of a petition
summary: A fragment of a petition to the dux of the Thebaid(?).  The fragment of the subscription on verso is illegible. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Philosophical Treatise
summary: On the recto beginnings of 5 lines in a different hand (from P.Fay.311A); on the verso parts of 7 lines in the same hand as the verso of P.Fay.311A

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for a payment in wheat (?) to the pra(k)t�r sitik�n of Theadelpheia.

title: Copies of various documents
summary: Copies of various documents; the first, which is headed antigraphon dikai�maton epikrise�s is an application from Thermouthion, the descendant of a katoikos, for the epikrisis of her son, whose birth was returned in the eighteenth year of Hadrian; the second is a copy of a kat' oikian apograph� addressed to Heirax, strategus, and Ti[magenes, basilikos grammateus], in A.D. 161-2; the third is a copy of a kat' oikian anti(graphon) Souchamm�nos, so probably all of the documents concerned him.

title: Letter from Chaer[emon?]
summary: Letter from Chaer[emon?] to his mother, requesting her to let part of a house, and announcing the dispatch of a key, etc.

title: Letter from Philoxenion to Theotimos
summary: 33 lines of a letter from Philoxenion to Theotimos (cf. P.Fay. 11-12).

title: Account
summary: On the recto account in three columns, two of which are nearly complete, mainly concerning chortos and arakos.  On the verso parts of four columns of a money account mentioning the thirtieth year (of Augustus)

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for a series of payments, generally 180 drachmae, in different months, into the bank of Demetrius for  zut�r.  Above these are beginnings of 4 lines in the same hand, and remains of a preceding column.  On the verso an account.

title: List of Persons
summary: List of persons, each line beginning with kl�r(ros?)and ending with kai Alex(andreias?) homoi�s.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for arith(m�tikou) kat(oik�n) of the fifteenth year.

title: Return
summary: Beginning of a return from the sitologoi of Magais.

title: Petition (?)
summary: Petition, which is, for the greater part, illegible, relating to one Sebastianos. The beginning is missing, having been cut by whoever used the verso. The recto seems to have been intentionally erased. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Account
summary: Account, practically complete.

title: Philosophical Work
summary: Parts of two incomplete columns of a philosophical work concerning the gods.

title: List of Payments
summary: Fragments of a list of payments in kind upon sphragides of land.

title: List of Land-holders
summary: Two fragments, containing on the recto part of a list of kl�(roi) numbered in order, with much abbreviated entries under each, apparently a list of the holders of parts of each kl�ros with the amounts of their farms.  On the verso part of a sitologos' account in several columns, giving a list of payments on different days.

title: Acknowledgement of Payment
summary: Acknowledgement by the sitologoi of Berenicis Aigialou of a payment by Flavia � of 10 1/4 artabae of wheat.

title: List of Abstracts/Letter
summary: On the recto a list of abstracts of contracts.  On the verso a letter from Ptolemais to Papirianus.

title: Lease
summary: Lease of a kl�ros at Theadelpheia, nearly complete, but much obliterated in parts.

title: Contract
summary: Contract appointing Dioscorus to sell at Alexandria a female slave on behalf of Aphrodous.

title: Homer, Iliad IV, 61-72
summary: A column from an Homeric text which was written on the back of a tomos sugkoll�simos of reports addressed to Zoilos also called �nos, strategos and dated to the reign of Antoninus Pius. The column covered the back of five of those reports, which were on uniform, tall, narrow strips of papyrus. The hand of the verso is a rather large, rough uncial of the late 2nd century.

title: Plato, Gorgias 522 C, D
summary: The present small fragment of the Gorgias shows a number of variations from the mss. text. Since the papyrus is broken off at both sides, the exact division of the lines is hypothetical. The number of letters in the lines which have been completed varies overall between 30 and 35. On the verso there are accounts.

title: Draft of petition
summary: Draft of petition addressed to the emperor Justinian from an inhabitant of Aphrodito, on behalf of his village. He asks for the right of autopragia to be restored to his village. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Literary fragment
summary: Author and subject of this fragment remain obscure. Several phrases refer to architectural details, and the presence of verbs in the future tense is noticeable. One might be tempted to regard this piece as a specification for a temple still to be erected, but the question in l. 16 points to a work like the Eikones of Philostratus of Lemnos. The length of the line is undetermined, but it appears to be considerable. On the verso are remains of an account of artabae.

title: Literary fragments (of a Romance?)
summary: Unidentified fragments, possibly of a Romance. Each fragment begins at the top of the column, and (a) is complete at the right-hand side. A dot above the line marks the division of works on three occasions. There are doubtful traces on the verso.

title: Virgil, Aeneid III, 444-468, with Greek translation
summary: This large fragment of papyrus carries verses 444-456 on the verso and 456-468 on the recto of Aeneid III, with a  Greek translation running on a parallel column. The papyrus is in a bad state of conservation. Most probably the page comes from a codex. The hand, in both languages, is a practiced cursive, assignable to the 4th-5th century.

title: Horoscope
summary: Horoscope of an individual whose name and date of birth are lost. As usual, the horoscope gives the position of the sun, the moon,  the five principal planets and the point on the horizon which was rising at the hour of birth of the subject. The writing is across the fibres in a neat uncial of the late 1st or early 2nd century.

title: Fragment of horoscope
summary: In this fragment of a horoscope the names of the planets stand farther than usual apart from the corresponding constellations. It may possibly be significant that the three signs of the Zodiac which are here grouped together (Virgo, Taurus, Gemini) all be belong to the oikos Hermou, while the two which stand alone on either side (Sagittarius and Capricornus) to the oikoi Dios and Kronou respectively.

title: Manifestations in honour of Vespasian
summary: This incomplete and badly conserved fragment, is written in a rather neat uncial, which however becomes more and more cursive as we approach the foot. The verso contains the remains of an account. The text appears to contain narrative, but it also reproduces acclamations in honour of Vespasian.

title: Copy of the edict of a praefect
summary: This copy was incorporated in a document that contained at least two columns. The fragment is in a very bad state of conservation, and stripping of the surface has obliterated parts of the writing.

title: Extract from an edict of T. Haterius Nepos
summary: The papyrus is preserved in six vertical strips, breaks occurring where the sheet was originally folded. On the right one or two more strips are missing. The hand is a large neat and rather carefully executed cursive. The extract comes from an edict which must have been one of the first that T. Haterius Nepos issued when he came in office

title: Receipt of a graph� hiere�n
summary: Peteuris, dean of the priests of Socnobrasis, addresses the annual report of his temple to the strategos of the Herakleidou meris.

title: Receipt of a graph� hiere�n
summary: Ammonios, son of Onnophris, priest of Socnobrasis, addresses the same report as in P.Fouad 11 to the basilikogrammateus of the Herakleidou meris.

title: Petition
summary: Petition presented to an unknown ecclesiastical dignitary, from the monks of the convent of Psinepois, who were being wronged by the pagarch of Antaiopolis, Menas. Large cursive. The recto and the verso are entirely covered by writing. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of a petition
summary: Some priests of Socnobrasis are writing to the basilikogrammateus of the Herakleidou meris asking for exemption from the liturgy of working on dikes.

title: Fragment of a financial report
summary: Fragment of an account of receipts and expenses connected to graph� and cheirismos. It seems that this has to do with the payment of taxes.

title: Census return
summary: This census declaration is written out by an unknown person on behalf of the illiterate proprietor of one half of a house in Arsinoe. It quotes from the census of A.D. 104 and 118 (which it refers to as 'the past year'). On the verso: P.Fouad 31.

title: Report to the strategos
summary: Beginning of a report to the strategos from an ibis-feeder of the Sarapeion of Oxyrhynchus. The papyrus breaks before the subject of the report can be ascertained, but the part that remains is not without interest. The papyrus was folded four times from right to left, and is badly shredded at the folds.

title: Report to the strategos
summary: The superintendents of the pasture dues report to the strategos that they have taken no revenue between  the first and the fifth of the month. The papyrus is incomplete at top and bottom. On the verso is a fragment of a 2nd or 3rd century account.

title: Undertaking with oath
summary: The oath is sworn by six presbuteroi d�mosi�n ge�rg�n of the village of Oxyrhyncha. The undertaking concerned is the prevention of the inundation of the drumoi.

title: Undertaking with oath
summary: The oath is sworn by five presbuteroi d�mosi�n ge�rg�n of the village of Ares. The undertaking concerned is the prevention of the inundation of the drumoi.

title: Oath concerning surety
summary: The papyrus is incomplete at the top, and damaged in the middle. The details of the surety offered therefore are to a large extent irrecoverable, but there seems to be a connection to a future undertaking of a liturgy.

title: Proceedings of a hearing
summary: The papyrus is complete apart from some damage to its left side, l. 6 and below. The text starts with a list of the committee before which the discharged soldiers would appear, and then continues with the ruling they received.

title: Report of judicial proceedings
summary: Only the opening of this report of judicial proceedings is preserved, but it has the interest of presenting to us Dius, son of Zeuxis, who appears in several documents of the Fouad collection. There is undeciphered writing on the verso.

title: Beginning of a contract
summary: The beginning of a contract agreed with Flavius Helladius, scriniairius in the offices of the dux of Thebaid. A certain Menas had been reduced by poverty to give up the freedom of his youngest daughter, in return for a nomisma. After her father's death, the elder sister, Martha, following the profession of salt fish merchant, sought the help of Helliadus to buy back her younger sister - who her master claims the right to keep.

title: Lawsuit about a will
summary: The papyrus, of which four detached fragments remain, is inscribed both on verso and recto. The line is apparently a long one. On the recto, three columns can be distinguished. The verso bears two columns only, written upside down in relation to those on the recto. From the use of the word diatagma it seems justifiable to infer that these fragments deal with a lawsuit about a will. The verso appears to deal with the same case: the hand is very similar, only somewhat more cursive.

title: Petition to a praefect
summary: In this very inaccurate copy of an interesting document the writing runs along the fibres. The papyrus has been folded into three from top to bottom and into four vertically. The document is addressed to Marcus Sempronius Liberalis.

title: Petition concerning forty drachmae
summary: The part of the papyrus which is mutilated deprives us of the circumstances in which Bassus was to repay to Harpocration the value of two doors and forty drachmae of silver. What still remains is an account of his various attempts to recover his property,

title: Petition concerning  an attack
summary: Aurelius Ammonius sends a petition to the office of the strategus to complain against the unknown persons who caused his son's head injury. The text is a further indication that by the 3rd century the villages situated at the edge of the desert were no longer irrigated by the Nile.

title: Hypomnema concerning a house
summary: Tasionis alias Dionysia and Ammonius each own a house that is connected to that of the other. Next to their property there is a very old construction that seems about to collapse, and they demand that the owners repair it, or else they would be held responsible for any damages suffered if it did collapse.

title: Fragment of a petition
summary: Verso of P.Fouad 15.

title: Demand to open a will
summary: The text is complete and well preserved. It contains a two-line subscription. According to the normal procedure, the necessary steps have already been taken (the copy of the testament has been presented, the witnesses have appeared, and the tax has been paid.

title: Donation because of death
summary: In the form of a simple homologia, Tapeteuris, with the assistance of her husband, makes provisions for after the death of the latter, concerning his possessions and the upbringing of their son.

title: Deed of divorce
summary: The document is an agreement to dissolve a suggraphie trophitis marriage. The youth of the parties of the divorce is noteworthy; the husband is 19, the wife presumably younger.

title: Power of attorney
summary: According to the first part of this document, the text would seem to be about a power of attorney concering private affairs, while the second part is relative to judiciary affairs. Thaesis gives full power of attorney to her husband Ptollion to manage some of her private financial affairs, and also to be her agent in a process against Thoonis and others.

title: Imperial rescript
summary: Imperial rescript addressed to the dux of the Thebaid, ordering a grant of reclamation of the village of Aphrodito, if an inquiry shows that its claim to be autopraktos is correct. On the verso, a rhythmical prayer written in sloping uncial. Below the prayer a fragment of the rescript preserved on the recto is repeated in a finer cursive. Apart from this fragment on the verso, we also possess a third example of the same text. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment of power of attorney
summary: This fragment is well preserved, but it only represents one third of the original document. The details of this document are unclear; Sarapias alias Dionysia gives power of attorney to [�] alias Eutyches, for the purpose of opening a will and other matters.

title: Contract of apprenticeship
summary: By this cheirographon, Menodorus undertakes to teach Fuscus the craft of the seated weaver.

title: End of an oath
summary: Only the end of the document is preserved. Asclepiades swears an oath forwill also have the rest of his property confiscated. Philiscos, to whom he is ceding a part of his property, that he will respect the contract between them. Should he breach his promise, he will suffer all the agreed penalties and will also have the rest of his property confiscated.

title: Agreement
summary: This papyrus contains the end of an agreement concerning property in Oxyrhynchus about the middle of the 3rd century. There are some difficulties of reading, including variations from common formulae.

title: Lease of land
summary: Agreement for lease of a parcel of grass-land already sown and under cultivation. The lease is for 8 1/2 months. The loss of the ends of the lines leaves some details in the dark. The text of the contract is in a rapid cursive, and the subscription of the lessor in rough, medium-sized uncials. The break in the papyrus is fairly regular.

title: Agreement
summary: A complicated agreement relating to three men and containing a surety clause. The precise details of the agreement are obscured by the lacunae in the papyrus and the difficulty of the hand.

title: Farming agreement
summary: This fragment of an agreement concerns the farming of land at Sbennytus in the Fayum, one party belonging to the quarter of the Second Goosefarm at Arsinoe, the other perhaps to The Macedonians, also at Arsinoe. A note in cursive Greek, 12 lines,  mainly illegible, at the left side of the papyrus, contains the name of Sbennytus and possibly [Dion]ysius.

title: Lease of land
summary: An unusually detailed lease of land, dealing with two parcels of land. The village Phokoou is mentioned, which lay in the 5th pagus of the Oxyrhynchite nome, in the Eastern Toparchy.

title: Antichretic loan
summary: A contract drawn up in the form of sugch�r�sis, addressed to the archidikastes of Alexandria. One of the two parties is a Roman citizen, as it often happens in this type of documents. The object of the contract is an antichresis with right of habitation to cover the interest of the loan.

title: Loan of 50 denarii
summary: The body of the document is written in Latin, but the layout follows the Greek formula of the cheirographon. There are four subscriptions, three of which are in Greek. The loan was repaid, since the document is crossed out.

title: Marriage contract
summary: From cartonnage found in Abusir el meleq. Large, unattractive hand, very abraded. Over the text the remains of three lines in a different hand, probably the outline for a marriage contract, which however is not connected with the one of the main text.

title: Imperial rescript
summary: Second example of 67024. The variants it presents have been indicated in the notes of the latter. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Fragment concerning a loan of gold to goldsmiths
summary: The top of this document is missing, and it is impossibe to estimate its original size. One banker and several goldsmiths are involved, but none of their names are preserved.

title: Loan of money
summary: Loan of 240 drachmae from Patron to Cronion.

title: Loan of money and wheat
summary: The papyrus is incomplete on the left, where 3-4 letters are missing at the beginning, while at the foot, in the last lines, up to 12 letters are lost. The writing is an upright, regular cursive. Caius lends 200 drachmae and 110 artabae of wheat to his brother Heracleides and his wife. The second part of the document sets out the conditions for this loan.

title: Loan of money and barley
summary: Petosiris borrowed 248 drachmae and [�] artabae of barley from Harpocration. The conditions and provisions for the case that the loan should not be repaid are not entirely clear, but they include some kind of antichretic arrangement. In any case the loan was repaid, since it is crossed out.

title: Loan of money
summary: Fragment of an homologia where two joint debtors acknowledge a debt of 280 drachmae. The papyrus is incomplete on the left and at the foot.

title: Loan of money
summary: Copy of a homologia where Deios and his brother Tyrannos alias Isidorus acknowledge their joint debt of 496 silver drachmae to Hippalos.

title: Loan of money
summary: The papyrus is missing the top and foot. [..H?]orion acknowledges the receipt of a sum of at least 2 talents, probably for the purchase of wheat, from a high official of Oxyrhynchus, whose name is lost. The loan is to be repaid within the same year.

title: Formula of receipt
summary: On the recto there are no traces of letters above line 1. On the verso after chairein which apparently begins a similar formula, uncertain traces of letters remain. To this formula the actual receipt would add at the beginning the names of those concerned, and at the end the date of the transaction.

title: Account of a debt
summary: This document, written on the verso of P. Fouad 77, begins with the statement of a debt. Then, after some obscure details, it continues in the form of a letter addressed to a particular individual.

title: Receipt for rent of farmland
summary: The sheet of papyrus is of considerable dimensions, but the writing actually occupies just the top half. It is a receipt of rent for farmland owed to Ptolemaeis, for the years 9-11 of Hadrian.

title: Imperial rescript
summary: Second example of 67026, which is an imperial rescript addressed to the dux of the Thebaid, ordering that justice be restored in the case of a man called Dioscoros and his sister whose fortune had been wrongfully seized. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipt for rent of lodgings
summary: Acknowledgement of receipt of rent for lodgings, paid partly in money and partly in kind.

title: Receipt for repayment of a loan
summary: Maron has received from Callinicos and his wife(?) the sum of 1288 silver drachmae in repayment of a loan which he had made to them. Only the last ten lines of the document are very legible.

title: Receipt for a deposit
summary: Acknowledgement of receipt on deposit of four artabs (probably of wheat). The papyrus is complete, but it is full of holes, so that much of the text is lost. The writing occupies only the upper third of the sheet.

title: Extract of a census register
summary: This extract from the register of personal descriptions (eikonismos) of the 8th year of Vespasian was doubtless appended to another document to substantiate a statement or claim made therein.

title: Certificate for work on the embankments
summary: Five scribes took part in the writing of this certificate. In l. 8 the word ses�mei�mai is not contracted, as it usually is, but is written in full. The document has been folded twice.

title: Certificate for work on the embankments
summary: In the present certificate, three scribes have writen: the first official who drew up a number of forms valid for the whole year, a second, who added the exact date, the place and the workman's name, and finally the katasporeus who signed the document. The document has been folded twice.

title: Certificate for work on the embankments
summary: Either the certificate is in the same hand throughout, or the second hand begins at l. 6.

title: Certificate for work on the embankments
summary: This certificate has been folded twice, vertically.

title: Receipt for part of the expenses of an epit�r�sis
summary: Horion and his associate epit�r�tai acknowledge receipt from Kainonos of his share of the expenses of the epit�r�sis. Kainonos must therefore have been one of the epit�r�tai himself. The reason why this receipt was issued him by his colleagues is not entirely clear. The papyrus was folded vertically, from left to right, four times.

title: Customs receipt
summary: This small square of light yellow, thick papyrus contains a receipt for the familiar customs dues of 1/100 and 1/50 which were levied on produce entering or leaving a town. The hand is a rather large, coarse scrawl of the early 2nd century: the 17th year is probably that of Trajan.

title: Imperial rescript
summary: An imperial rescript addressed to the dux of the Thebaid, on the subject of Dioscoros. The plaintiff's father, having remarried, has made to his second wife a gift of the goods which constituted the dowry of the first - whose son, Dioscoros, has brought proceedings against his stepmother for the restitution of his inheritance. The Emperor commands the dux of Thebaid to reduce to just proportions the share of the second wife, in conformity with a law of King Leon the First, which is, in part, cited at the end of the papyrus. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Tax receipt
summary: Only the beginning is preserved of this receipt issued by the praktores argurik�n of Hephaistias. The papyrus was folded five times vertically from right to left.

title: Account of taxes
summary: The exact nature of this document is unclear. The text starts with the name of Heracleides the royal scribe, followed by the date and the formula that introduces the monthly accounts of wheat. Then on the verso, the scribe seems to be indicating the total amount of artabae of wheat received by the fiscus and the temple administration, before returning to the recto and adding, upside down, one more note concerning the receipt of a further 814 artabae of wheat.

title: List of tax payments
summary: Payments of 4 drachmae and multiples of 4 are common, but the amount alone is not enough to determine which tax it is.

title: Extract from a tax register
summary: The papyrus is an extract from the register of taxes on the transmission of goods (egkuklion). In its main clauses it reproduces a contract of sale. The papyrus is incomplete on the right. The writing, frequently abraded, is fast and very cursive.

title: Register of tax-payments
summary: This list of names appears to give, after the name of each man, the names of his father, his father's father, his mother and his mother's father. That this is the middle portion of a register of tax payments is evident because of the symbol for drachma preserved in l. 16.

title: Fragment of a list
summary: This puzzling fragment of a detailed list refers to Aphrodite; if this is meant as a place name, it may be one of three villages, two in the Fayum (one in the division of Polemon, one in that of Heraclides), and one in the Antaiopolite nome. At first glance one conjectures that only a narrow strip is missing at the left, but in fact the lines may have been originally of considerable length.

title: List of names
summary: This list of names with particulars (perhaps payments made by villages) is written on the verso, the recto being blank. After the first name details of some kind appear to follow in the next three lines, as is the case with the second name, in l. 5; then the names appear to be given line by line. The papyrus has four horizontal creases.

title: List of objects to buy in Alexandria
summary: Phoibammon, a public scribe (nomikos), gives his brother a list of things to buy in Alexandria. The purchases are described preceisely, but in interpreting them some details remain obscure.

title: Letter of Thaubas
summary: A woman named Thaubas writes to her father to let him know that her sister Herennia died, after giving birth prematurely to a stillborn child, and to ask him to come.

title: Imperial rescript
summary: An imperial rescript, addressed to several functionaries -  to the dux and to the praeses.  An unknown person has addressed a plea to the Emperor about the conduct of a certain Theodosios, who has collected the taxes of the village of Aphrodito but has kept them for his personal use. In the text, the rescript is interestingly referred to as the epistole (l.13). Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Invitation to a sacred diner
summary: Sarapous sends an invitation for a dinner in honour of Isis. The invitation is written on a small rectangular piece of papyrus, which is completely intact and folded into two.

title: Private letter
summary: The letter starts by referring to a number of dispatched items, and then mentions a few matters to be taken care of by the recipients. P. Fouad 54 is written on the verso of this letter.

title: Letter to Philon
summary: Fragment from the conclusion of a letter to Philon mentioning an enclosure.

title: Private letter
summary: The recipient has neglected sending the writer of the letter some olive-oil that the latter had asked for, and now he is writing demanding for his oil to be dispatched to him immediately or else.

title: Letter from the doctor Eudaimon
summary: Eudaimon, a doctor, sends a long letter to his mother and his brothers to ask for their news. He is worried because they had to go through the same troubles that he and his family had to endure. When he comes to the details however the numerous lacunae obscure the meaning, but it is clear that he was having judiciary difficulties, perhaps to do with an inheritance. The language is correct and reveals a high level of erudition. The verso contains an address, which is almost entirely effaced.

title: Private letter
summary: Of this rudely scrawled and barbarously ungrammatical letter, little can be said, and much remains obscure. The beginning seems to be: To Cythra from Uran(ius) [or Urani(a)] all�

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: This letter is preserved on four joining fragments, which form approximately the middle of the letter. The papyrus was of bad quality, and the verso was repaired using pieces of a different colour. What is not typical letter-writing formulae (ll. 7-16) relates to the dispatch of objects, notably perfume. The lacunae and bad spelling prevent one from understanding precisely what the writer wants to say. The writing is neat but inelegant.

title: Fragment of a letter
summary: The beginning is missing, as is a wide strip on the right. The right part of the papyrus appears to have been burned. It has been folded six times horizontally. The letter relates to the dispatch of objects.

title: Private letter
summary: On a large piece of papyrus, four lines of writing by an elegant hand, in a tall, sloping script. The sender asks that several maulakia be sent to him. The papyrus was folded ten times vertically. An address on the verso.

title: Letter of Paul to his brother
summary: Paul writes to his brother to reproach him for bad behaviour. The language is often wrong, and the sense is not always clear. The person in question is a notarios called Samuel. The letter was folded into four when the ink on the verso was still fresh.

title: Order
summary: Order addressed by the praeses to the inhabitants of Aphrodito, to send the wheat destined for the annona to Alexandria. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter to the surerior of the Monastery of Penitence
summary: John writes to George, the superior of the Monastery of Penitence, with great respect and humility. He appears to be a monk of the community of Aphrodito. He wants to inform the superior of the intrigues caused by a dangerous monk. On the verso, at right angles, there is the address.

title: Letter to the surerior of the Monastery of Penitence
summary: The letter, written by Andreas, an abbot, to George, the superior of the Monastery of Penitence, aims to inform the latter of the troubles in the Monastery of Stratonikis concerning their previous superior Jeremiah. Address on the back.

title: Letter to the surerior of the Monastery of Penitence
summary: A humble monk named John who belonged to the community of Aphrodito sends his greetings to George, the superior of the Monastery of Penitence, and asks him to remember him in his prayers. This is a good example of the effusive style of Byzantine epistolography.

title: Letter to the surerior of the Monastery of Penitence
summary: Psoios, a monk, writes to the superior of the Monastery of Penitence to express his respect and filial devotion. The syntax and spelling of the letter are very bad.

title: Sale of Ground
summary: The sale of a piece of ground at Apollinopolis Magna (Edfu) to Aurelius Paamius and Aurelius Syrus in the seventh year of the emperor Tiberius II Constantinus.

title: A Will
summary: Part of a will, written in a well-formed cursive hand.  On the verso is a document written in tachygraphy.

title: Homer, Iliad, xxi-xxiii
summary: Fragments of the twenty-first, twenty-second and twenty-third books of the Iliad.  There are occasional corrections, sometimes apparently by the scribe himself, sometimes in a smaller and probably different hand.

title: Homer, Iliad, xxi-xxiii
summary: One of a series of fragments of the Iliad, Books xxi-xxiii.  In all there are parts of about 190 lines, a  number of which affords a sufficiently accurate estimate of the general character of the text.

title: Homer, Iliad, xxi-xxiii
summary: One of a  series of fragments of the Iliad, Books xxi-xxiii.  In all there are parts of about 190 lines, a  number of which affords a sufficiently accurate estimate of the general character of the text.

title: Homer, Iliad, xxi-xxiii
summary: One of a series of fragments of the Iliad, Books xxi-xxiii.  In all there are parts of about 190 lines, a  number of which affords a sufficiently accurate estimate of the general character of the text.

title: Abstract of a Case for Trial
summary: A short summary of the details of a judicial suit, but owing to lacunae and the involved construction the situation is not easy to grasp.  The text, according to a common custom at this period is given in duplicate, but there is a gap in the middle, and unfortunately the commencement is defective in both copies. Thrason and Pasis, the parties in the case, seem each to have accused the other of having lost 7 jars of wine from a store-place which had been leased by the owner Pasis. Affidavits were entered on both sides, and evidence was given that the store had been opened. The nature of the judgement, if indeed a judgement is recorded by the papyrus at all, depends upon the view taken of a mutilated passage, but there is reason to think that Pasis was condemned to pay compensation to Thrason to the extent of 56 drachmae.

title: Property-Return of Sheep
summary: An apograph� of sheep, drawn up by a military settler.

title: Petition to the King
summary: A petition to Ptolemy (Euergetes) from Antigonus, probably a phylacites, complaining that Patron, the archiphylacites of the lower toparchy of the Oxyrhynchite nome, had prevented him from carrying out his duties, and asking for redress.  P.Hibeh.I.073 is a letter from Antigonus on the same subject to Dorion, the epistates. Both documents are mutilated, but they supplement each other, and the sequence of events is clear. Callidromus, a Cyrenean settler, had obtained unlawful possession of a donkey belonging to a certain Dorion, and Antigonus has been directed by Dorion the epistates to compel Callidromus either to restore the animal to its owner or to pay its value. Antigonus accordingly arrested Callidromus and lodged him in a prison at the village of Sinaru. Patron then intervened, and not only released Callidromus from prison but himself took possession of the donkey.

title: Letter of Dionysodorus (?) to Ptolemaeus
summary: A letter to Ptolemaeus ordering a person who had brought the writer a petition to be sent to him.  The writer's name is doubtful, but is perhaps Dionysodorus.

title: Letter of Criton to Plutarchus
summary: A letter from Criton asking Plutarchus to settle accounts, in order that Criton might meet a demand to pay for some seed which had been sown upon a cleruchic holding.

title: Letter of Asclepiades to Clitarchus
summary: A short letter from Asclepiades, directing Clitarchus to come to him bringing an account and the balance of some money.

title: Correspondence concerning a Strike
summary: A fragment of a series of official letters concerning a strike of slaves employed in a stone-quarry.  Lines 4-11 contain a copy of a letter from Antiochus to Dorion forwarding a letter from Aenesidemus, of which only the beginning is preserved, and ordering the immediate arrest of the offenders. Lines 1-3 are the conclusion of a letter which may be from Dorion to the phylakitai.

title: Letter of Nicias to Argaeus
summary: A letter to Argaeus froom Nicias requesting that two persons should be released from some public service, the nature of which is not specified.  As the scene was Alabastropolis, it was probably connected with quarrying.  The writer and addressee no doubt occupied official positions, but there is no indication of their rank.

title: Official Correspondence concerning Cleruchs
summary: This papyrus, along with P.Hibeh.I.082, belong to the correspondence of Asclepiades, an official of some importance in the Arsinoite nome in the 9th year (of Euergetes).  This papyrus contains a series of letters from Artemidorus, giving information of the death of certain cavalry soldiers, and directing that possession of their holdings should be resumed by the government. The language of Artemidorus plainly implies that the reversion of such to the State at their owner's death was the usual course at this period. That fact was not before definitely ascertained, though it had been inferred from the apparent inability of cleruchs to dispose of their holdings by will.

title: Receipt for Rent
summary: A receipt for a large quantity of barley delivered by Euphranor to a superior official as rent of cleruchic land.

title: Decree
summary: A decree of the dux of the Thebaid, forbidding functionaries of the tribunal to take more than two keratia as sportulae from plaintiffs. The aforesaid ruling will have to be translated into coptic. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Taxing List
summary: Three fragments of a long taxing-list, perhaps written at the logeut�rion of Phebichis, recording money payments for various taxes at different villages of the K�it�s by individuals who are in most if not all cases the tax-payers, not the tax-collectors.

title: Contract for a Loan
summary: Conclusion of a contract for the loan of 18 3/4 artabae of olyra from Zenodorus to Menonides.  On the verso some possibly related lines.

title: Part of a letter to an official
summary: Part of a letter to an official mentioning the chief-priest at Phebichis Petosiris the son of Hari�t�s.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for 20 drachmae paid by Petosiris, agent of Ta�mbes, for  zut�ra, 11 drachmae being on account of Pachon, to Nikolaos trapezit�s and Stoto�tis dokimast�s at Phebichis.

title: Homer, Iliad, ii
summary: A portion of a roll of Iliad ii, torn so that the top half of the roll is preserved for the full width of one column, and across parts of the two adjoining columns.

title: Career of Tiberius Julius Alexander
summary: These two non-contiguous scraps are of interest for one reason only, that they add to the evidence for the career of the well-known prefect of Egypt, Tiberius Julius Alexander, the information that he was subsequently promoted to the praetorian prefecture.

title: Declaration by Shippers
summary: This text, which was found floating near the town, S.E. wall, has been reconstituted from a number of fragments.  Of its two columns, the first preserves only a few letters from the ends of lines written in three different hands.  In the second column two
provenance: 1903     floating near the town, S.E. wall

title: Sale of nine Column-Drums with Bases and Capitals
summary: Memorandum from two inhabitants of Ancyronon to the archiereus and probably other authorities of Herakleopolis, recalling the writers' offer to sell nine columns plus moulded bases and uncarved capitals at a total price of 2376 silver drachmae, and requesting that the tamias be instructed to pay out the sum named so that the stone-work can be delivered and erected.

title: Taxing List of Heracleopolite Villages
summary: The value of this text lies in its geographical information about the villages of the Heracleopolite nome.  Since many of the names are known to be of villages in the Ko�te toparchy, it is probable that the list is confined to that toparchy.  The recto contains parts of three columns of official account in which the name of the village precedes the name of the individual payer. The verso was re-used for a tax account of sums (in money?) arranged by villages.

title: Assessment for anabolika
summary: This mutilated text contains a declaration made by two persons whose official description is lost, under the heading ] linou anabolikou and is apparently an assessment of linen piece-articles (lina) to be produced in the year 309/10 by the linen-weavers of Ancyronon and surrounding villages (representing a district or pagus of the Heracleopolite nome?).

title: Decree
summary: A decree of the dux of the Thebaid, forbidding functionaries of the tribunal to take more than two keratia as sportulae from plaintiffs. The aforesaid ruling will have to be translated into coptic. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipt for Hay
summary: Receipt issued by two superintendents of the public bakery at Babylon to the comarchs of Ancyronon for 803 pounds of hay, being the amount due on 406 arourae over a four-month period for levy for workmen for 6 days and for maintenance (trofei'on).

title: Prose Literary or Christian Fragment
summary: On the verso of an official letter or document, in a large hand of the later second century A.D.  The verso hand is minute (there are 15 lines in a bare two inches of height) and the appearance of this tiny capital, which slopes slightly to the right, suggests a Christian text.

title: Petition
summary: Numerous fragments of a petition, in a very brittle condition.

title: Memorandum or Petition
summary: Left-hand piece from the beginning of a memorandum or petition addressed to a number of quasi-municipal officers, possibly of Heracleopolis.

title: Petition
summary: On the verso of P.Hibeh.II.216.  Ends of lines of draft of a petition to an important Roman, perhaps a patron of an athletic sunodos.

title: Official Letter
summary: Ends of lines of a first column and beginnings of lines in a second, perhaps from an official letter.  The subject concerns corn transport.

title: Latin Letter of Recommendation
summary: First five lines of a letter in Latin.  The text mentions an orthographus leg( ) n( ) (which is perhaps to be expanded leg(ionis) n(ostrae) rather than leg(ati) n(ostri), though the rank of orthographus legionis is unknown), and palaeographically for the use of a short overwritten horizontal stroke (not unlike an accent) as a substitute for the n in praesens.

title: Contract of Loan
summary: Parts of 19 lines from the end of what may be a contract of loan.  The subscription is quoted for the village name Choin�this (?), a village in the Coite toparchy.

title: Contract of Loan or depositum
summary: Ends of 15 lines of a contract of loan or depositum, the names of the parties being lost, and the sum illegible.

title: Corn Account
summary: Parts of two columns of a corn account.

title: Contract
summary: Contract agreed between Palladios, comes of the sacred consistory, and Epigonos, for one part, and Dioscoros, son of Apollos, Kallinikos, son of Victor, Apollos son of Ioannes, Kyros son of Victor, represented by Senouthos, son of Apollos, inhabitants of Aphrodito, for the other part. These have come to plead different cases at Byzantium, and have received satisfaction by an imperial decision, notably concerning the matter of autoprakton schema, recognised in their city. Palladios undertakes to accompany them on their return, as an executor negotii for a fee stipulated in the contract. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Register of House Property
summary: One fragment and a number of scraps of a register of house property.  Property is described under its owner's name (in the genitive), and is situated in various villages of the Heracleopolite nome.

title: List of Names
summary: Parts of three columns (and traces of a fourth) of a list of names, the majority being checked by two oblique strokes to the left.

title: Agricultural Account Book
summary: One large fragment, two smaller ones, and a score of scraps of an agricultural accounting book, in some ways analogous to the well-known farm accounts of Hermopolis.  On the verso of the main frgment are 15 lines of a day-to-day account in a hand of the later second century A.D. mentioning sums in obols and quantities in sphurid(ia).
provenance: 1902, found in the rubbish of the cemetery

title: Private Account
summary: On the verso of P.Hibeh.II.281, a private account.

title: Cloth Mummy Wrapper
summary: Cloth mummy wrapper inscribed Thenarpa�sios Phli�[..]i.

title: Correspondence concerning cleruchoi
summary: A record of the correspondence received and sent by Lamiscos, in the period between 16 Tybi and 13 Pachon, year 5.

title: Orders for distribution of seed
summary: Two orders for the distribution of seed to various individuals in various villages, written one after the other, in the same hand.  Signed Nikostratos.

title: Declaration concerning a theft
summary: Petesouchos, son of Pasis, informs Cresilaos that he has been the victim of violence and theft on the road from Tebetnou to Corphotoun(?).

title: Memoire of a royal cultivator
summary: A memoir addressed to Nicanor, perhaps a strategos, by Phibis, royal cultivator, complaining that Cresilaos has taken from him several animals that he needs for work on the land.

title: Naukleros Receipt
summary: The first of a series of four receipts of the Naukleros whose boats carried grain.

title: Tax receipt
summary: A tax receipt (100 nomismata) for the first levy of the chrysika of the first indiction, delivered to the magistrates of Aphrodito, and signed by Heliodorus, treasurer of the eparchy of the Thebaid. The cursive is competent and neat, and similar to that of 67033. Signature of the same hand as the signature of 67033. Illegible subscription on the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Loan in kind
summary: A loan of 50 artabs of seed to Menes, and another of 10 artabs, to Bromenos, an epigonos cleruch.  Entered in duplicate.  Dated 6 Athyr, Year 35.

title: Account of Woodwork for a House
summary: Account concerning the making and painting of the woodwork in a new house, probably built for Diotimos.

title: Tax List
summary: One of the large tax lists found in Karanis

title: Report of a hearing
summary: Report of a hearing held by Rullius Achilleus, epistrategos, concerning a complaint entered by Gaius Iulius Ptolemaios against Chairemon, who failed to appear and is to be sent to Antinooupolis for trial.

title: Declaration to officials
summary: Declaration addressed to several officials by a certain Sarapion, acting as a representative for 3 Antinoites.

title: Receipt
summary: Receipt for Secretary's Salary and Expenses

title: Register of work
summary: Register of work performed for the penthemeral dyke corvee

title: Receipts for poll-tax
summary: Roll bearing 2 receipts for poll-tax, 2 for garden taxes  and 2 for beer tax, all in the name of the same tax-payer, Petheus son of Phaesis.

title: Receipt for garden taxes
summary: Receipt for garden taxes paid in the name of a Marcus Anthestius Tertianus, with remnants of 2 other receipts.

title: Documentary papyrus.
summary: More than one(?) document, very abraded.

title: Tax receipt
summary: A tax receipt (114 nomismata 14 keratia) signed by Heliodorus, general treasurer of the eparchy of the Thebaid, for the third levy of the chrysika of the sixth indiction. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Receipts for garden taxes
summary: 5 receipts for garden taxes (hekte apomoira, geometria and others) probably all issued to one person, Gaius Iulius Niger.

title: Receipt for hay tax
summary: Receipt for hay tax (monodesmia chortou) issued to a certain Kastor.

title: Receipt of wine
summary: Receipt of wine for annona, issued to a certain Horion, written on a papyrus which originally contained Iliad II: the abraded beginnings of ll. 1-42 are visible on the left edge (upside down).

title: Receipt for Rent
summary: Receipt for Rent of Domain Land and Dues on epibolae.

title: Petition to the prefect
summary: Petition addressed to the prefect Quintus Aemilius Saturninus by Gemellus alias Horion, concerning violent trespass made on his property by two men.

title: Petition to the epistrategos
summary: Petition to the epistrategos Calpurnius Concessus by Gemellus alias Horion, concerning violence and tentative extortion made on him and his mother by a tax collector's assistant.

title: Petition to the epistrategos
summary: Petition to the epistrategos Arrius Victor by Gemellus alias Horion, requesting that his right to exemption from liturgical service be confirmed,

title: Copy of a contract
summary: Copy of a contract in which Gaius Iulius Gemellus, a veteran, agrees to cede some of his property to a minor named Chairemon.

title: Sale of a house
summary: Valeria Diodora sells a house and two courtyards inherited from her mother to Gaius Iulius Niger, a former cavalryman, for 800 drachmai

title: Register of work
summary: Register of work performed for the penthemeral dyke corvee

title: Tax receipt
summary: A tax receipt (114 nomismata, 8 keratia) for the third levy of taxes of the 4th indiction, signed by Victor, treasurer general of the eparchy of the Thebaid. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Petition
summary: Republished as ChLA XLII 1223.

summary: Letter from Apollonous to her brother (and husband?) Iulius Terentianus, explaining how in his absence she is managing his farms and keeping an eye on his children

title: Letter from Gaius Iulius Apollinarius to his mother
summary: Letter from Gaius Iulius Apollinarius to his mother Tasoucharion, expressing his homesickness and calming his mother's fears for his safety and comfort

title: Letter from Gaius Iulius Apollinarius to his father
summary: Letter from Gaius Iulius Apollinarius to his father Sabinus, complaining about his negligence as a correspondent and telling him news from his life in the legion and his hopes of getting an advancement

summary: Letter from Claudius Terentianus to his father(?), acknowledging receipt of certain articles of clothes, requesting military equipment, relating a petty quarrel, and referring to his enlistment in the (Alexandrian) fleet

title: Letter from Claudius Terentianus to his father(?)
summary: Letter from Claudius Terentianus to his father(?), concerning the things he has received from him, describing what he has sent to him and speaking about an illness which he suffered and about his hope of being transferred from the Alexandrian fleet to a cohort.

title: Letter from Claudius Terentianus to his father(?)
summary: Letter from Claudius Terentianus to his father(?), asking him to make a number of purchases for his mother

summary: Letter [probably from Claudius Terentianus to his father(?)], of uncertain content

title: Letter from Claudius Terentianus to his father(?)
summary: Letter from Claudius Terentianus to his father(?), explaining his unsuccessful attempts to obtain money and clothing to go to Alexandria

summary: Receipt issued to the demosios logos of Aphrodito by the tribune of Antaiopolis, on the receipt of  his annual salary. Subscription almost completely faded on the verso. Belongs to the archive of Dioscoros.

title: Letter from Claudius Terentianus
summary: Letter from Claudius Terentianus to Longinus Priscus

title: Letter from [Tabetheus(?)] to her brother
